The MAH was visited and a letter of invitation was issued. A consultative meeting was held with the manufacturer and it was established that the product is unregistered. The company was issued an administrative charge for sale and distribution of unregistered product.
outcome: THE CASE WAS REFERRED TO D R&R FOR A REVIEW OF THE REGISTRATION DOCUMENTS AND THE REGULARIZATION OF THE PRODUCT NAME VIZ A VIZ THE ACTIVE INGREDIENTS

complaint: Sale and distribution of Golden Cereal Maize and Soya Protein (NRN: 01-0469), a look-alike of Nestle Golden Morn. The product is manufactured by Prothea Limited located in Isele, Asaba, Delta State
observation: The information from the NAFDAC Registered Products Database showed that the NAFDAC number was assigned to Vina Table Water (01-0469L)  produced by Banex Industries Limited, Isolo. The certificate however expired in 2014. A memo was written to D PMS with a recommendation that the case should be forwarded to NAFDAC Office Delta State for further regulatory action.
outcome: AWAITING FEEDBACK FROM DELTA STATE OFFICE

complaint: Suspected unregistered Appedor Cyproheptadine with Lysine with unapproved composition.
observation: A preliminary investigation was carried out and it was discovered that the NRN assigned to the product belonged to Vitborah Cyproheptadine with Lysine registered by the same MAH (Vitborah). A team of PMS Officers visited the address of purchase, the product was found and mopped up. A consultative meeting was also held with the MAH and it was discovered that the company had applied for a change of product name which has been approved however it was further established that the MAH also changed the product formulation with Cyproheptadine having 4% in place of 2%. The MAH paid an administrative charge of 5 million naira for a change of formulation without approval. The MAH was directed to recall the product from circulation and the report of recall was submitted
outcome: ALERT NOTIFICATION HAS BEEN ISSUED FOR SURVEILLANCE ACROSS THE COUNTRY

complaint: Sale of Nutrilac Cereal containing maggots and worms by Justrite, Akowonjo Lagos

The products were mopped up and an invitation letter was issued to the company.                                                                              The batch detail and date markings were however not stated in the complaint which hinders adequate investigation however, the mopped-up batches were forwarded to the laboratory and the report of laboratory analysis was satisfactory
outcome: THE REPORT OF LABORATORY ANALYSIS WAS FORWARDED TO D (PMS)

complaint: Sale of unauthorized/prohibited items at Grandsquare Supermarket and Hartley's Supermarket

outcome: THE COMPLAINT PRODUCTS WERE NOT FOUND.
